title: The House That Jack Built
author: Traditional (illustrated by Randolph Caldecott)
edition: R. Caldecott's Picture Book, Frederick Warne & Co., 1878
note: Verbatim — the complete cumulative rhyme, one cumulative verse per spread (11 spreads, House → Malt → Rat → … → Farmer). Line breaks follow the Caldecott edition's picture-book typesetting (some poetic lines wrap mid-phrase); words are unaltered. Fills the cumulative-trope gap in the corpus.
vocabNote: Extreme repetition gives a distinctively low type-token ratio (~0.11) — by far the lowest in the corpus — because each verse restates every prior clause. A strong regression signal for the repetition path.
